guidelines_resources.notes: Uses five clinical factors to predict survival in patients with metastatic RCC.

key_trials.full_name: Pembrolizumab plus Axitinib versus Sunitinib for Advanced Renal-Cell Carcinoma
key_trials.population: Treatment-naïve advanced clear cell RCC, favorable-, intermediate-, or poor-risk, Karnofsky PS ≥70%.
key_trials.primary_endpoint: Overall survival and progression-free survival in PD-L1 positive and overall population.
key_trials.key_result: Axitinib + Pembrolizumab significantly improved OS (HR 0.68, 95% CI 0.57-0.81) and PFS (HR 0.69, 95% CI 0.59-0.81) vs. sunitinib. ORR: 60.6% vs. 39.6%.
key_trials.secondary_outcomes: Consistent benefit across risk groups. Updated 67-month follow-up showed continued OS benefit (median OS 47.2 vs. 40.8 months).
key_trials.practice_change: Established axitinib+pembrolizumab as a category 1 preferred first-line therapy for advanced RCC.

key_trials.full_name: Nivolumab plus Cabozantinib versus Sunitinib for Advanced Renal-Cell Carcinoma
key_trials.population: Treatment-naïve advanced clear cell RCC, favorable-, intermediate-, or poor-risk, Karnofsky PS ≥70%.
key_trials.key_result: Cabozantinib + Nivolumab significantly improved PFS (HR 0.58, 95% CI 0.49-0.70) and OS (HR 0.70, 95% CI 0.55-0.90) vs. sunitinib. ORR: 55.7% vs. 27.1%.
key_trials.secondary_outcomes: Benefit observed across all risk groups, including patients with sarcomatoid features.
key_trials.practice_change: Established cabozantinib+nivolumab as a category 1 preferred first-line therapy for advanced RCC.

key_trials.full_name: Lenvatinib plus Pembrolizumab or Everolimus for Advanced Renal Cell Carcinoma
key_trials.population: Treatment-naïve advanced clear cell RCC, favorable-, intermediate-, or poor-risk, Karnofsky PS ≥70%.
key_trials.key_result: Lenvatinib + Pembrolizumab significantly improved PFS (HR 0.39, 95% CI 0.32-0.49) and OS (HR 0.66, 95% CI 0.50-0.87) vs. sunitinib. ORR: 71.0% vs. 36.1%.
key_trials.secondary_outcomes: Final OS analysis at 49 months median follow-up showed significant OS benefit (median OS 53.7 vs. 54.3 months, HR 0.79, P=0.0424).
key_trials.practice_change: Established lenvatinib+pembrolizumab as a category 1 preferred first-line therapy for advanced RCC.

key_trials.full_name: Nivolumab plus Ipilimumab versus Sunitinib in Advanced Renal-Cell Carcinoma
key_trials.population: Treatment-naïve advanced clear cell RCC; intermediate- or poor-risk (co-primary endpoint), favorable-risk (exploratory).
key_trials.primary_endpoint: Overall survival, objective response rate, and progression-free survival in intermediate/poor-risk patients.
key_trials.key_result: Ipilimumab + Nivolumab significantly improved OS (HR 0.69, 95% CI 0.59-0.81) and ORR (42.4% vs. 27.5%) in intermediate/poor-risk patients. No significant OS benefit in favorable-risk patients initially.
key_trials.secondary_outcomes: 8-year follow-up showed trend towards improved OS in favorable-risk patients (HR 0.82, 95% CI 0.60-1.13).
key_trials.practice_change: Established ipilimumab+nivolumab as a category 1 preferred first-line therapy for intermediate/poor-risk RCC.

key_trials.full_name: Adjuvant Pembrolizumab after Nephrectomy in Renal-Cell Carcinoma
key_trials.population: Localized RCC with intermediate or high risk of recurrence (stage II grade 4, stage III, or M1 NED) after nephrectomy.
key_trials.key_result: Pembrolizumab significantly improved DFS (HR 0.68, 95% CI 0.53-0.87, P=0.002) and OS (HR 0.62, 95% CI 0.44-0.87, P=0.005) vs. placebo.
key_trials.secondary_outcomes: Benefit observed across risk subgroups. 57-month follow-up confirmed DFS and OS benefit.
key_trials.practice_change: Established adjuvant pembrolizumab as a category 1 recommendation for intermediate/high-risk clear cell RCC after nephrectomy.

key_trials.full_name: Cabozantinib versus Sunitinib as Initial Targeted Therapy for Patients with Metastatic Renal Cell Carcinoma of Poor or Intermediate Risk
key_trials.population: Intermediate- or poor-risk metastatic clear cell RCC, ECOG PS 0-2.
key_trials.key_result: Cabozantinib significantly improved PFS (HR 0.66, 95% CI 0.50-0.87) and ORR (33% vs. 12%) vs. sunitinib.
key_trials.secondary_outcomes: OS favored cabozantinib but not statistically significant (HR 0.80, 95% CI 0.50-1.26).
key_trials.practice_change: Established cabozantinib as a category 2A preferred first-line option for poor/intermediate-risk RCC.

key_trials.full_name: Cabozantinib versus Everolimus in Advanced Renal-Cell Carcinoma
key_trials.population: Advanced clear cell RCC with progression after prior VEGFR-TKI.
key_trials.key_result: Cabozantinib significantly improved PFS (HR 0.51, 95% CI 0.41-0.62) and OS (HR 0.70, 95% CI 0.58-0.85) vs. everolimus.
key_trials.practice_change: Established cabozantinib as a subsequent therapy option after prior VEGFR-TKI.

key_trials.full_name: Belzutifan versus Everolimus for Advanced Renal-Cell Carcinoma
key_trials.population: Advanced clear cell RCC progressed after 1-3 prior therapies including PD-1/L1 and VEGF-TKI.
key_trials.key_result: Belzutifan significantly improved PFS (HR 0.75, 95% CI 0.63-0.90, P<0.001) and ORR (21.9% vs. 3.5%).
key_trials.secondary_outcomes: OS not significantly different (HR 0.88, 95% CI 0.73-1.07, P=0.20).
key_trials.practice_change: Established belzutifan as a category 2A subsequent therapy option after prior IO and VEGF-TKI.
